title: Runtime Profiles And Check Matrix
summary: AgentCanon ships broad shared surfaces, but not every surface is mandatory for
every repository task. Treat root views and tools as installed capability, then
activate only the profile required by the current change.

profile_classes.required_when: An agent performs or reviews repo work
VS Code devcontainer or agent ergonomics are used
Dockerfile, image, pack, Jupyter, or container setup changes
GitHub Actions, PR automation, or GitHub path-constrained copies change
Experiment topics, formal runs, result summaries, or research workflows change
C or C++ code, build layout, or native artifacts change
User asks to persist memory, feedback/retrospective is observed, or agent-learning is in scope
AgentCanon maintenance, repo-wide audit, or scheduled cleanup work

compatibility_note: Compatibility surfaces such as legacy subtree routes may remain documented, but
only under the matching compatibility profile. They are not the default path for

risk_classes.examples: Link/text edits in project-local docs with no source contract change
risk_classes.required_validation: docs check for touched docs and changed-file dependency header checks

risk_classes.examples: Narrow Python or shell edit with local tests
risk_classes.required_validation: changed-file dependency checks, targeted tests, ruff/pyright when Python changes

risk_classes.examples: Docker, GitHub, experiment, C++, devcontainer, MCP, or memory surface change
`vendor/agent-canon/`, root shared views, skills, workflows, hooks, tools
risk_classes.required_validation: `make agent-canon-pr-check` or equivalent AgentCanon PR gate

risk_classes.examples: repo-wide rewrite, workflow redesign, broad policy change, or user-requested comprehensive run
risk_classes.required_validation: run bundle, dependency review, focused and full validation gates, independent review

risk_note: Static analysis and reading evidence are the primary validation evidence for
AgentCanon tasks. `make ci` remains the full local confidence gate, but it is
not the default or only acceptable evidence for every owner-bounded change. Use
operation checks, smoke runs, full CI, long test suites, benchmarks, experiments,
and other broad execution as supplemental evidence when runtime behavior,
integration risk, or unresolved static/read findings require them. The selected
validation must match the changed paths, owner surface, and risk class, and the
PR or run bundle must state why that set is sufficient.
Prompt-only or prose-only edits use the surface-specific docs, prompt, eval,
and dependency checks selected by the active profile; they do not automatically

rule: After any validation test/check failure, do not simplify, revert, delete intended behavior/tests, weaken the oracle, or downscope required validation just to pass.
First record the five machine fields: `failing_contract`, `observation_level`, `cause_classification`, `intent_preservation`, and `evidence`.

This runtime-profile inventory JSON is the canonical validation-failure-response taxonomy owner. `documents/runtime-profiles-and-check-matrix.md` is the generated reader projection, while `agents/canonical/CODEX_WORKFLOW.md`, `agents/canonical/CODEX_SUBAGENTS.md`, `agents/TASK_WORKFLOWS.md`, and `documents/REVIEW_PROCESS.md` are workflow, handoff, reader-map, or checklist projections that must cite this inventory instead of defining separate slug lists.
Repair with approved intent preserved or escalate before intent change.
repair_routes: repair_same_intent: repair the owning code, config, docs, workflow, fixture, environment, generated artifact, test oracle, or residual evidence route while preserving approved intent
redesign_same_intent: return to design/test planning while preserving the same approved intent
escalate_design_conflict: escalate approved-design/user-request conflict before any intent change

required_check: broad quick CI with already-run docs/workflow gates skipped

check_matrix.changed_surface: Root shared views or submodule pin

required_check: relevant GitHub Actions evidence when available
managed run evidence for formal experiment changes
project-native configure/build/test and C++ reviewer/checker where applicable
append-only artifact evidence and improvement guide/eval checks when prompts or logging fields change

closeout_rule: Do not turn optional profiles into hidden mandatory work. If a profile is not
activated by the changed paths, user request, or risk class, record it as
`not_applicable` instead of running or explaining its checks.
